title: equisetum × rothmaleri
intro: Equisetum × rothmaleri, also known as Rothmaler's horsetail, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family and is a result of the cross between Equisetum telmateia and Equisetum limosum. The medicinal properties of Equisetum × rothmaleri lie in its high silica content and its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent. The plant has several health benefits, including being used to treat urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other kidney-related issues, as well as being used to reduce inflammation and improve bone health. Equisetum × rothmaleri can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to create topical creams and ointments for wound healing.
